items.hook_line: You can tell whether a barbecue shop is worth sitting down for by the first grill tray, not by the sign outside.
items.target_scene: street-side charcoal barbecue, lamb skewer shops, visible grill counters, night-market barbecue stores
items.cover_direction: Use a hero grill shot with signature skewers, then support it with a small storefront or outdoor-seating corner.
first_3_shots: signature skewers turning and getting seasoned
fresh handoff to the table or first bite
items.fallback_if_missing: If grill access is missing, switch to a fresh-handoff route and stop claiming a strong live-fire hook.

items.hook_line: If a barbecue shop is really hot at night, the proof is outside the door and on the tables, not in the copy.
items.target_scene: late-night street barbecue, outdoor-seating barbecue, busy after-work barbecue bars, seafood barbecue stalls
items.cover_direction: Frame the storefront and occupied tables together so the audience reads real night-service status at a glance.
first_3_shots: real guest arrival or waiting shot
server delivering skewers, drinks, or refills
items.fallback_if_missing: If no real queue or peak-hour footage exists, pivot to a hero-food or group-fit hook instead.

items.hook_line: People do not remember barbecue shops by the lights. They remember the skewers every table orders twice.
items.target_scene: lamb skewers, chicken wings, grilled waist, mixed skewer trays, signature meat-heavy barbecue shops
items.cover_direction: Let the hero skewer fill the cover, then support it with a menu marker or whole-table context.
items.fallback_if_missing: If there is no stable hero item, move to a night-scene or group-social route.

items.hook_line: Turn, fire control, and seasoning rhythm explain barbecue skill better than any voice-over ever will.
items.target_scene: visible grill masters, open grill lines, seasoning-heavy barbecue shops, hand-grilled skewer concepts
items.cover_direction: Use the grill master's hands and the hero skewer as the main composition, with charcoal or spice trays as support.
items.fallback_if_missing: If grill-master process footage is unavailable, switch to a finished-item experience hook.

items.hook_line: For oysters and shellfish, the audience only believes freshness when the handling and grill stages are visible.
items.target_scene: seafood barbecue, oyster grills, scallop-focused stalls, shrimp and shellfish night-market barbecue
items.cover_direction: Split the cover between seafood handling and a hot finished plate so freshness and appetite support each other.
items.fallback_if_missing: If prep proof is missing, stop leading with freshness and switch to flavor or late-night social value.

items.hook_line: A barbecue shop wins when the table can keep sitting, drinking, and ordering, not just when one skewer looks good.
items.target_scene: barbecue bars, after-work hangouts, outdoor night seating, group-friendly late-night stores
items.cover_direction: Lead with a whole table of skewers and drinks, then support it with face-safe social interaction.
first_3_shots: server handoff followed by shared eating
items.fallback_if_missing: If there is no real group table, switch to a single-table tasting or hero-skewer route.

items.hook_line: Value in barbecue is not a price tag. It is whether the full table actually looks worth ordering.
items.target_scene: combo-heavy barbecue stores, community barbecue, two-person sets, group platters, beer-plus-skewer bundles
items.cover_direction: Use a visibly full table or platter-heavy spread that makes quantity readable before the title is even finished.
items.fallback_if_missing: If only single-item footage exists, stop pushing value-combo language and return to hero-item proof.

items.hook_line: The strongest barbecue comfort signal is not no smoke at all. It is smoke, service, and table care staying in balance.
items.target_scene: indoor barbecue bars, low-smoke concepts, family-friendly barbecue, longer-stay dining formats
items.cover_direction: Pair a clean table with active grilling and a small service detail such as grate change or empty-skewer clearing.
items.fallback_if_missing: If comfort proof is weak, do not use this hook. Switch back to food or social proof.
